titleStmt.title: SII 18.105: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 105. (B.K. No. 30 of 1932-33.) DĒVAGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Broken slab on the slope of a hill called Gireguḍḍa.

summary.p: This inscription is dated in the Chālukya-Vikrama year 2[6], Chitrabhānu, Chaitra śu. Bidige, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The details are irregular. The tithi cited corresponds to A.D. 1102, March 22. The week-day was Saturday and there was no saṁkrānti on that day.

It states that Tribhuvanasiṅgi-Paṇḍita, who was governing Dēvaṅgēri, Tammuge, Kaḷasūru and other divisions, made a grant of land for the god Tribhuvanēśvara installed by him. It also refers to a tank built by him to the east of Tammuge. The grant was made into the hands of the Twelve Dēvaputras. It is interesting to note that one of the verses gives iconographical details of Bhairava.
A certain Chavuṇḍa, the Sandhivigrahi of Tribhuvanasiṁgi-Paṇḍita seems to have been mentioned at the end.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: (A few lines in the beginning are lost.)

kti kaḷāviḷāsama
ṇamaṁ taḷedirddu negarttevetta
saṁgītaśāḷākauśaḷatuṁga janā
rasiṁgaṁ parōpakārasurathanī ś®ṁgi muḷivange parabaḷadi ś®ṁgi virōdhigaḷige ś®ṁgiyeni
ppī tribhuvanasiṁgiya daṭaroḷare samarāṁgaṇadoḷ © svasti yamanimamasvādhyāya
dhyānadhāraṇamōnānuṣṭhāṇajapasamādhiśīḷaguṇasaṁpannarappa śrīmatṛbhuvanasiṁgipa
ṇḍitar śrīmad baṁkāprarada śrī mumaṁ dēvaṁgēri ta
mmuge kaḷasūrumumaṁ prabhusāmyadiṁdāḷu
ttamirddu © svasti śrīmaccāḷukyavikramavarṣada 2 6 neya citrabhānu saṁvatsarada caitra śuddha
bidige sōmavāraduttarāyaṇasaṁkrāṁti vyatīpātadaṁduśrīmatribhuvanēśvaradēvara
pratiṣṭheyaṁ māḍi dēvaraṁgabhōgakkaṁ pāvuḷakkaṁ khaṁḍasphuṭitajīrṇnōddhārakkaṁ tapōdhanarāhāradānakkaṁ
pannirbbarddēvaputrargge biṭṭa datti dēvaṁgēriya bijanakeṟeya keḷage dē biṭṭa tōṁṭa vasu
ge mattareraḍu Ā tōṁṭada mūḍa dēvara nivēdyakke gadde kaṁbavayvattu ka
piḷageṟeyiṁ keḷage ku paḍuva Aṭṭakeyiṁ baḍaga Alli dē
varige biṭṭa gadde matta reraḍu kisumattaroṁdu Antu matta
raydu Ā keṟeyiṁ keḷage hulbeṭṭa dōṇiyiṁ mūḍa ma
ṇalageṟeyiṁ paḍuva tammugeyiṁ baḍagalalli dēvarige biṭṭa gadde matta
reraḍu kisumattaroṁdu Antu mattaru mūṟu tammugeyiṁ mūḍa tribhuvanasiṁgipaṁ
ḍitaru kaṭṭisida keṟeya keḷage tribhuvanēśvaradēvargge biṭṭa tōṁṭa vasuge matta
roṁdu Alliṁ paḍuvalere mattaru mū dēvargge biṭṭa bhūmiyalu pu
ṭṭida konnoḷage dēvaputrakaru haṁnirvvarige gadyāṇavirppattanālku Antāpaṁnni
rvvaruṁ baḷi yiṁ benakana beṭṭadiṁ teṁkapolamēreyiṁ mūḍalere mattaru ha(ṁ)nne
raḍu dēvaṁgēri tammugeya tōṭadeleya kēṟige vīsamuṁ dēvadōṁṭadaḍakeyā yamuṁ
Eleya mottamuṁ dēvaṁgēriyalu mārida taḷabhaṁḍakke koṟage baṁdu mārida bhaṁḍakke poṁnirvva
ḷa bhattamuṁ dēvara naṁdādīvigegoṁdu gāṇamu mikka gāṇada soṁṭage yeṇneyuṁ maṭada keṟeya
keḷagoṁdu pūdōṭamaṁ biṭṭarintī dharmmavanārōrvvar pratipāḷisidargge vāraṇāsi kurukṣētra prayāge
yargghyatīrtthadalu sahasra kavileya kōḍuṁ koḷagumaṁ paṁcaratnadalu khaciyisi caturvvēda
pāragarappa brāhmaṇarggubhayamukhigoṭṭa phalamakku yī dharmmamanārōrvvaraḷida raḷidavargge
vāraṇāsi kurukṣētra prayāgeyargghyatīrtthadalu sahasra kavileyuṁ sahasrabrāhmaṇarumaṁ
[A]ḷida paṁcamahāpātakanakku svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasundharā ṣaṣṭhirvvarṣa sahasrāṇi
viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi © na viṣaṁ viṣamityāku dēvasvaṁ viṣamu cyatē viṣamē kākinaṁ haṁti
dēvasvaṁ putrapautrikaṁ sarvvacharddhi śunaṁ bhakṣyē śunacharddhi na bhakṣayēttēsya dēvadhanam
bhuktvānarakaṁ kōṭi bhuṁjayē © Aḷidavanidanerkkōṭiyanaḷidaṁ tīrtthaṁgaḷe
nitumanitumanōrantaḷidaṁ paśuvaṁ pārvvaranaḷidaṁ meyyaḷidanātanajagujagiḷida ©
Asukṛdara Aḷkadāḷkuduregaḷ verasārdidirāge tōṟane nosaluri(r)gaṇ tṛśūḷa phaṇibhūṣaṇa
bhīṣaṇa ruṇḍamāḷe kūrasi ghanaghaṁṭikāḷi kuḍudāḍe maruḷvadeyeṁba cihnamaṁ
visasanaraṁgadoḷkaramagurvvisalī karavāḷabhairava © Asidaṇḍaṁ lātadaṇḍaṁ ri
ṣṭakilā
ppi tribhu
karmmakō
ktanuṁ tāmaṟi
kadoḷ kāḷidāsa
bhāvisi kaṇḍaripeḍe
nasiṁgipaṁḍitara saṁdhivigrahi cavuṁḍamaThe continuation is lost.
Digital edition of SII 18.105 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
